raw_response: {"summary":"One new feature-search hypothesis from live research: test unsupervised sky-local photometric residualization as a distinct preprocessing family, motivated by SDSS extinction and calibration effects.","hypotheses":[{"title":"Sky-Local Photometric Residualization","summary":"Create covariate-only sky-sector calibration features by expressing each object's magnitudes and colors relative to local alpha/delta neighborhood medians.","feature_family":"sky_local_photometric_residual_features","feature_strategy":"Keep raw ugriz, redshift, alpha/delta, spectral_type, galaxy_population, and basic adjacent color indices, then add unsupervised sky-local residual features. Partition the sky using fixed RA/Dec bins or spherical KMeans/HEALPix-like cells on combined train/test covariates. For each band and key color, compute global medians and sky-cell medians, optionally smoothed with neighboring cells. Add residuals such as u_minus_cell_median, g_r_minus_cell_median, cell_minus_global offsets, local robust scale ratios, and small-cell count/log-count features. Build these maps without target labels; for CV, fit sky maps from fold-train plus test covariates and apply to fold-validation rows.","baseline_model_panel":"Use a consistent simple multiclass panel: balanced linear/logistic model for additive residual signal, shallow class-weighted random forest or extra-trees for nonlinear cell interactions, and one class-balanced gradient-boosted tree model such as LightGBM/CatBoost when runtime allows.","model_panel_rationale":"This panel separates whether the residualized representation is linearly useful, only useful through nonlinear sky-color interactions, or merely redundant with existing raw magnitudes and colors, while keeping algorithm choice secondary to the preprocessing test.","validation_strategy":"Use 5-fold StratifiedKFold on `class` and balanced accuracy. Compute all sky-local medians/counts with no labels and preferably fold-aware covariate fitting to avoid optimistic validation from validation-row distribution statistics.","materialization_hint":"Stage the implementation as: build base photometric/color features, create deterministic sky cells from alpha/delta, compute fold-aware covariate-only median and robust-scale tables for bands/colors, join residual and offset features back to train/validation/test, then evaluate the same balanced model panel against the base feature set.","expected_signal":"If useful, CV should improve most for tree models and show feature importance on sky-cell offsets/residual colors, with gains concentrated in classes whose observed colors are affected by local calibration/extinction-like shifts. Linear performance may improve modestly if residuals remove broad spatial bias.","novelty_confidence":"medium","risk":"Overlap risk with prior sky-coordinate formulas exists because both use alpha/delta, but this is a different preprocessing mechanism: local photometric residualization rather than raw sky bins/densities/harmonics. Small sky cells can overfit or create noisy medians; using test covariates transductively is label-safe but may make CV slightly optimistic if not fold-aware.","sources":["https://arxiv.org/abs/0706.0369","https://arxiv.org/abs/1512.01214","https://arxiv.org/abs/astro-ph/0408504"]}]}

parsed_response.summary: One new feature-search hypothesis from live research: test unsupervised sky-local photometric residualization as a distinct preprocessing family, motivated by SDSS extinction and calibration effects.

hypotheses.title: Sky-Local Photometric Residualization
hypotheses.summary: Create covariate-only sky-sector calibration features by expressing each object's magnitudes and colors relative to local alpha/delta neighborhood medians.
hypotheses.feature_strategy: Keep raw ugriz, redshift, alpha/delta, spectral_type, galaxy_population, and basic adjacent color indices, then add unsupervised sky-local residual features. Partition the sky using fixed RA/Dec bins or spherical KMeans/HEALPix-like cells on combined train/test covariates. For each band and key color, compute global medians and sky-cell medians, optionally smoothed with neighboring cells. Add residuals such as u_minus_cell_median, g_r_minus_cell_median, cell_minus_global offsets, local robust scale ratios, and small-cell count/log-count features. Build these maps without target labels; for CV, fit sky maps from fold-train plus test covariates and apply to fold-validation rows.
hypotheses.baseline_model_panel: Use a consistent simple multiclass panel: balanced linear/logistic model for additive residual signal, shallow class-weighted random forest or extra-trees for nonlinear cell interactions, and one class-balanced gradient-boosted tree model such as LightGBM/CatBoost when runtime allows.
hypotheses.model_panel_rationale: This panel separates whether the residualized representation is linearly useful, only useful through nonlinear sky-color interactions, or merely redundant with existing raw magnitudes and colors, while keeping algorithm choice secondary to the preprocessing test.
hypotheses.validation_strategy: Use 5-fold StratifiedKFold on `class` and balanced accuracy. Compute all sky-local medians/counts with no labels and preferably fold-aware covariate fitting to avoid optimistic validation from validation-row distribution statistics.
hypotheses.materialization_hint: Stage the implementation as: build base photometric/color features, create deterministic sky cells from alpha/delta, compute fold-aware covariate-only median and robust-scale tables for bands/colors, join residual and offset features back to train/validation/test, then evaluate the same balanced model panel against the base feature set.
hypotheses.expected_signal: If useful, CV should improve most for tree models and show feature importance on sky-cell offsets/residual colors, with gains concentrated in classes whose observed colors are affected by local calibration/extinction-like shifts. Linear performance may improve modestly if residuals remove broad spatial bias.
hypotheses.risk: Overlap risk with prior sky-coordinate formulas exists because both use alpha/delta, but this is a different preprocessing mechanism: local photometric residualization rather than raw sky bins/densities/harmonics. Small sky cells can overfit or create noisy medians; using test covariates transductively is label-safe but may make CV slightly optimistic if not fold-aware.
